description: Annotation-layer distance measurement between two canvas positions using at() coordinates. Draws end markers and an optional centered text label on the annotation layer. Use this for measurements outside of 2D graphs (e.g. between write blocks, images, or canvas positions). For measuring shapes inside 2D graphs, use measure() instead.
utility: Create distance measurement annotation between two canvas positions outside of 2D graphs

arguments.name: from
arguments.description: Start position on the canvas using at() coordinates

arguments.name: to
arguments.description: End position on the canvas using at() coordinates

arguments.name: color
arguments.description: Color for measurement line and markers

alternates.syntax: distancemarker(at(r1, c1), at(r2, c2), "d", buff(-1.5), c(green))

examples.scenario: Distance marker showing vertex distance with label and fadeout
Distance marker showing foci distance with offset buff and fadeout
Distance marker between two absolute positions for building height
Distance marker with custom text label and buff for max height
Distance marker with text label and color
